definition: Services-exporting EM economies face structural current account erosion from AI automation. India IT services ~$200B/yr (~6% GDP); Philippines BPO ~$33-35B (~8% GDP). McKinsey estimates 60% of current BPO tasks automatable by 2030-2040. NDF forwards do not embed this structural shift. However, the 'sudden stop' framing was refuted — this is gradual comparative advantage erosion over 5-25 years, not a Calvo-style crisis.
evidence: India IT services ~$200B/yr (~6% GDP); Philippines BPO ~$33-35B (~8% GDP)
McKinsey: 60% of BPO tasks automatable by 2030-2040
NDF forwards do not embed this structural shift
Philippines has $100B reserves, IG rating, flexible exchange rate — not crisis-prone
pair_2 partially refuted sudden stop framing
open_questions: Can India and Philippines diversify services exports fast enough to offset AI automation?
At what automation penetration rate does the current account impact become visible in FX markets?
Does the NDF market's failure to embed this risk create an asymmetric opportunity?
